---
title: "Agents overview"
description: "Pick the right pack — tradeoffs, provider, auth, and install in one table."
---

Lowkey ships **eight agent packs**. Each one installs a different AI coding agent with a consistent deploy model (CFN or Terraform), security posture (profiles), and non-interactive install flow.

## Pick at a glance

| Pack | Stability | Provider | Auth | Agent model |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [openclaw](/agents/openclaw) | stable | Bedrock (configurable) | IAM role | Stateful gateway + persistent memory |
| [claude-code](/agents/claude-code) | stable | Bedrock | IAM role | Anthropic's coding CLI |
| [codex-cli](/agents/codex-cli) | experimental | **OpenAI** (not Bedrock) | `codex login` post-install | OpenAI's coding CLI |
| [kiro-cli](/agents/kiro-cli) | experimental | **Kiro cloud** (not Bedrock) | SSO or API key | AWS agentic IDE client |
| [nemoclaw](/agents/nemoclaw) | experimental | Bedrock (via bedrockify) | IAM role | OpenClaw in OpenShell sandbox |
| [hermes](/agents/hermes) | experimental | Bedrock (via bedrockify) | IAM role | NousResearch CLI |
| [pi](/agents/pi) | experimental | Bedrock (via bedrockify) | IAM role | Minimal terminal harness |
| [ironclaw](/agents/ironclaw) | experimental | Bedrock (via bedrockify) | IAM role | Rust-based NEAR AI agent |

<Info>
**Stable** = the pack is production-quality and won't change auth / API shape without notice.
**Experimental** = expect rough edges; upstream agent may break our pack between releases.
</Info>

## If you're not sure

- Want a **persistent agent with chat history, channels, and a gateway** → [OpenClaw](/agents/openclaw)
- Want **Claude Code on Bedrock** as-is → [Claude Code](/agents/claude-code)
- Want **Codex (OpenAI)** for one-shot execs → [codex-cli](/agents/codex-cli)
- Want **Kiro CLI** with MCP servers → [kiro-cli](/agents/kiro-cli)
- Want **maximum isolation** (sandboxed Bedrock agent) → [NemoClaw](/agents/nemoclaw)

## How agents differ

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Bedrock vs provider API">
    Bedrock-native packs (openclaw, claude-code, nemoclaw, hermes, pi, ironclaw) authenticate via the instance's IAM role — no API keys to manage. Provider-API packs (codex-cli, kiro-cli) need post-install auth (browser login, stored token, or Secrets Manager).
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="With gateway vs pure CLI">
    OpenClaw installs a systemd gateway on port 3001; other packs are pure CLI binaries invoked directly. This affects what "using the agent" means — OpenClaw has a long-running service; the others run per-invocation.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="bedrockify dependency">
    hermes, pi, ironclaw, and nemoclaw depend on **bedrockify** — a local OpenAI-compatible proxy that translates OpenAI-style API calls into Bedrock `InvokeModel`. Those packs install bedrockify as a dep automatically.
  </Accordion>
  <Accordion title="Data volume size">
    Stateful packs (openclaw, nemoclaw) get an 80 GB data volume. Stateless CLI packs (claude-code, codex-cli, kiro-cli, hermes, pi, ironclaw) get 0 GB.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Same CLI, different packs

Every pack uses the same non-interactive install flow:

```bash
curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
  --pack <pack-name> \
  --profile <builder|account_assistant|personal_assistant>
```

Pack-specific flags (like `--kiro-from-secret`) are documented on each agent's page.
